hero.title: Between class chats, fake news & AI
hero.lead: Digital situations are part of growing up today. Everything happens fast online—and children have to deal with a lot. Every day, they have to decide what to share, believe, comment on, or forward.
hero.leadEmphasis: Do you also worry about what your child encounters online—and how they deal with it?
hero.leadEmphasis2: With the learning app Amy Surfwing, your child develops digital literacy.
hero.bannerAlt: Insight into the story world of Amy Surfwing

learnMain.title: What children learn with Amy Surfwing
learnMain.intro: Together with Yasmin, Igor, and their class, children experience typical situations they may encounter online themselves.
learnMain.intro2: They learn what can happen—and how to deal with it.
learnMain.howTitle: How does Amy Surfwing achieve this?

0.title: Children understand how the digital world works
0.intro: Many online risks are difficult for children to understand.
0.learnTitle: With Amy Surfwing, they learn for example:
learnPoints: Why platforms try to capture their attention
What’s behind likes, trends, and viral posts
What data is shared when posting or taking photos
How algorithms shape what they see
Why some content is designed to be especially emotional or engaging
0.howIntro: Amy Surfwing teaches these connections not in a dry way, but within the story.
howPoints: All content is embedded in an ongoing story that addresses these topics.
The format is interactive and offers many ways for kids to explore topics, such as articles and audio content.
Child-friendly language, engaging questions, and playful elements help keep their attention and make the content stick.

1.title: Children learn to protect themselves
1.intro: Children explore online with curiosity—but often lack experience to recognize risky situations early.
1.learnTitle: With Amy Surfwing, children learn for example:
learnPoints: What to do in cases of cyberbullying
1.howIntro: The story and supporting materials show step by step how children can protect themselves.
howPoints: What to watch out for
How to avoid uncomfortable situations
How to react wisely in difficult moments

2.title: Children reflect on their own behavior
2.intro: Online, things happen quickly, publicly, and often under social pressure. With Amy Surfwing, children learn one key thing: think first, then click.
2.learnTitle: Children apply the characters’ decisions to their own lives:
2.howIntro: Amy Surfwing offers small, practical reflection prompts instead of overwhelming content.
howPoints: Short open questions with direct feedback
Only a few minutes per day: one focused impulse instead of overload
Practical everyday tips
Multiple-choice scenarios with motivating feedback

3.title: Children learn to see different perspectives
3.intro: In chats and social media, opinions often form quickly—even without knowing the full story.
3.learnTitle: With Amy Surfwing, children learn to:
learnPoints: Not blindly follow waves of outrage
3.howIntro: The story makes social dynamics visible and understandable.
howPoints: Children follow a school class with diverse personalities.
They learn about different perspectives and group dynamics.
They learn through interactions—not abstract rules.

4.title: Children understand consequences of their actions
4.intro: Online, things are quickly posted or shared. But what seems harmless can have lasting effects.
4.learnTitle: With Amy Surfwing, children learn to:
learnPoints: Pause before posting and consider consequences
Recognize when content can harm others
Understand that content can stay online for a long time
4.howIntro: Amy Surfwing combines reflection with real-life situations.
howPoints: Daily reflection questions linked to the story
Feedback from Coach Amy
Scenario-based exercises to understand consequences

5.title: Children recognize group pressure
5.intro: Group pressure can be strong—especially online.
learnPoints: Recognize when a group turns against someone
5.howIntro: The format makes group dynamics emotionally understandable.
howPoints: The story is chat-based
Characters create or resolve conflicts
Diary entries show emotional impact
Articles show better solutions

6.title: Children recognize manipulation and deception
6.intro: Not everything online is what it seems.
learnPoints: Understand how content can simplify or radicalize
6.howIntro: These topics are deeply integrated into the story.
howPoints: The story follows a structured narrative arc
Entire episodes focus on manipulation
Characters experience realistic examples
Fake news and deepfakes appear across seasons

whyApp.title: Why should my child use this app?
whyApp.intro: Amy Surfwing teaches digital literacy through story, reflection, and real-life situations.

0.title: Story instead of lessons
0.text: Children experience media literacy instead of just learning rules.

1.title: 5 minutes instead of endless screen time

5.title: Safe space
5.text: No social sharing, local data only.

ai.title: What role does AI play?
ai.intro: The stories of Amy Surfwing are developed by real authors. The images in the story are created using AI. A special feature of Amy Surfwing: our owl Amy uses AI to support children in thinking and reflecting.
ai.description: Amy can pick up on thoughts, mirror perspectives, and encourage further thinking. It does not diagnose or make psychological assessments.

safeAi.title: Safe AI
safeAi.text: The AI works within clear pedagogical guidelines. It does not make personal evaluations, give therapy recommendations, or replace conversations with adults.

ai.control: You decide for yourself whether and how the AI should be active.
ai.introShort: Our owl Amy uses AI to support children in thinking and reflecting. The stories are developed by real authors.

on.title: On
on.text: The AI is active and supports reflection moments in the story. It responds contextually – not constantly, but where it is pedagogically meaningful. On older or less powerful devices, it may respond more slowly.

auto.title: Auto
auto.text: The AI supports only when it is useful and technically stable. This is the recommended setting for most devices.

off.title: Off
off.text: The AI is turned off. The story still works fully and remains pedagogically sound – just without AI reflection prompts.

ai.changeHint: You can change the mode at any time.
ai.performanceHint: If the app responds slowly or takes longer to load, we recommend "Auto" or "Off".

items.0: Start together with the first episode – after that your child can continue independently.
items.1: Ask: "What would you do – and why?" and also: "How do you feel about it?"
items.2: Sharing the experience helps more than bans: talk about situations, not blame.
items.3: If you wish, choose the AI mode (On/Auto/Off) to suit your child.

items.0: Progress and settings are stored locally on the device – not in a cloud.
items.1: If the device is changed, reset, or lost, progress may be gone.
items.2: This is intentional: we do not use accounts and collect no personal user data.
items.3: The images in the app are created with AI support.

items.0: No time-consuming preparation: topics are already presented in a child-friendly way.
items.1: Short units: the daily episodes only take a few minutes.
items.2: Clear structure: the stories address typical situations from digital everyday life.
items.3: Child-friendly explanations: even complex topics like fake news or data protection are explained accessibly.
items.4: Conversation starters arise naturally through questions and story situations.

warn.title: Conversations remain the most important part
warn.body: Amy Surfwing can provide prompts. When something is troubling, a calm conversation usually helps most.

overview.whatBody: The new chat series for kids in an interactive story world. The learning app for media literacy, where kids think through situations online.
overview.forWhoBody: Recommended age: children between 10 and 12 years

principles.1: ⏱️ 5 minutes instead of endless screen time
principles.5: 🔒 Safe space instead of a social network

transparency.body: Amy Surfwing is intentionally not a social network. Content is not shared publicly. Progress stays locally on the device.

blog.title: Between class chats, fake news & AI
blog.body:
Online situations from children's everyday lives.
For parents who want to guide their children through the digital world.
